name: retry
description: A standard-library-only retry helper with exponential backoff and context cancellation. Retry(ctx, attempts, base, fn) retries fn on error with a doubling delay (base, 2*base, 4*base, ...), returning nil on first success or the last error once attempts are exhausted; it stops early and joins ctx.Err() if the context is done before or during a backoff wait. A generic Do[T] form returns a value, and nextDelay saturates the doubling to avoid duration overflow.

maintain.request: Add a WithMaxDelay functional option so callers can cap the exponential backoff: no single wait between attempts should exceed the supplied duration, even though the delay keeps doubling up to that cap. Thread the option (variadic, backward compatible) through both Retry and Do.

review.bug: The final-attempt guard uses `if i == attempts` instead of `if i == attempts-1`, an off-by-one that never fires (i only reaches attempts-1), so after the last failed attempt the code still creates a timer and sleeps a full backoff delay (and needlessly blocks on the context) before returning the error.
keywords: off-by-one in the final attempt guard
i == attempts can never be true
break never executes on the last iteration
extra backoff sleep after the last failed attempt
unnecessary delay before returning the error
